steps.title: Identify the relationship between the clauses
steps.content:
The first clause establishes a general observation—that time did not actually stand still, meaning something occurred over 252.7 million years.

The second clause provides the specific explanation for what that something was: natural processes removed sedimentary material from the stratigraphic record, creating the unconformity.

The transition word must show how the second clause refines or clarifies what the first clause means.

steps.title: Match the right transition
steps.content:
”Rather” refines a general or vague statement with precise, specific detail—it shifts from the broad claim that something happened (“time didn't stand still”) to the particular geological mechanism that actually happened (“processes removed material”).

The other options break down: however would signal an unnecessary contrast where none exists, moreover would treat the second clause as an additional fact rather than a clarification, and for instance would introduce an example rather than the specific explanation.

That confirms B is correct.
